justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified — 99.9% Onfido dependency makes 99.95% team SLA unachievable, with specific downtime numbers (8.7h vs 4.4h). (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended: async KYC with provisional registration, secondary KYC provider failover, and SLA revision. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as must-have blocker. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling issue identified with recommendation to decouple /health from /ready. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to MJ (architecture) and Bird (business rules) for SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is precise and presented as the central showstopper — not buried in a list

Escalations correctly routed to architecture (MJ) and domain (Bird) for the SLA product decision
Goes beyond rubric requirements: identifies PII/compliance risk, idempotency gap, thundering herd scenario, rate limiting gap, and API version pinning
Recommendations clearly prioritized into must-haves, should-haves, and nice-to-haves
Async KYC with provisional registration recommended as the pragmatic path forward
